Charles I.iftchild said he had only
heard of one person who really OPP
posed the franchise.


A delegation of citizens from the
North Side were present to urge the
franchise for the Nichols avenue part
of the line. This delegation included
DIR. II. G. Mauzey of o2315 Latah
street. p. Hughes, William Walmsley,
a. f. Krueckel and c. l.. Gilbert


The North Siders have arranged
for a mass meeting to boost the
franchise application to be held next
Saturday night in the new building
at Indiana avenue and Ash street
The councilmen from tho Fourth
ward have been invited and Council
man Snyder has announced that he
will be there.


Announcement was made last night
by President Boyd that definite ac-
ton on the franchise will be taken
by the council next Tuesday evening.


A call has been issued by President
Boyd for the first meeting of the
council to consider charter amend
ments to be held next Monday night
